title: Config
description:
The following is a list of all top-level bar config options.

These options can either be written at the very top object of your config,
or within an object in the [monitors](#monitors) config,
depending on your [use-case](#2-pick-your-use-case).

anchor_to_edges.description:
Whether to anchor the bar to the edges of the screen.
Setting to false centers the bar.

**Default**: `true`

autohide.description:
The duration in milliseconds before the bar is hidden after the cursor leaves.
Leave unset to disable auto-hide behaviour.

**Default**: `null`

center.description:
An array of modules to append to the center of the bar.

**Default**: `[]`

double_click_time.description:
The time in milliseconds to wait for a double-click.
Can be set to a number (e.g., `250`) or `"gtk"` to use GTK's setting.

**Default**: `250`

end.description:
An array of modules to append to the end of the bar.
Depending on the orientation, this is either the bottom or right edge.

**Default**: `[]`

exclusive_zone.description:
Whether the bar should reserve an exclusive zone around it.

When true, this prevents windows from rendering in the same space
as the bar, causing them to shift.

**Default**: `true` unless `start_hidden` is set.

height.description:
The bar's height in pixels.

Note that GTK treats this as a target minimum,
and if content inside the bar is over this,
it will automatically expand to fit.

**Default**: `42`

icon_overrides.description:
Map of app IDs (or classes) to icon names,
overriding the app's default icon.

**Default**: `{}`

icon_theme.description:
The name of the GTK icon theme to use.
Leave unset to use the default system theme.

**Default**: `null`

ironvar_defaults.description:
A map of [ironvar](ironvar) keys and values
to initialize Ironbar with on startup.

**Default**: `{}`

# Example

The following initializes an ironvar called `foo` set to `bar` on startup:

```corn
{ ironvar_defaults.foo = "bar" }
```

The variable can then be immediately fetched without needing to be manually set:

```sh
$ ironbar get foo
ok
bar
```

layer.description:
The layer-shell layer to place the bar on. Taken from the [wlr_layer_shell](https://wayland.app/protocols/wlr-layer-shell-unstable-v1#zwlr_layer_shell_v1:enum:layer) definition: > These values indicate which layers a surface can be rendered in. > They are ordered by z depth, bottom-most first. > Traditional shell surfaces will typically be rendered between the bottom and top layers. > Fullscreen shell surfaces are typically rendered at the top layer. > Multiple surfaces can share a single layer, and ordering within a single layer is undefined. **Valid options**: `background`, `bottom`, `top`, `overlay`
**Default**: `top`

margin.description:
The margin to use on each side of the bar, in pixels.
Object which takes `top`, `bottom`, `left` and `right` keys.

**Default**: `0` on all sides.

# Example

The following would set a 10px margin around each edge.

```corn
{
    margin.top = 10
    margin.bottom = 10
    margin.left = 10
    margin.right = 10
}
```

monitors.description:
A map of monitor names to configs.
Monitor names can be supplied in two formats:

- Connector names (`DP-1`, `HDMI-2`)
- Descriptions (`ASUSTek COMPUTER INC PA278QV M4LMQS060475`).
  A `starts_with` is applied allowing you to omit part of the description if convenient.

The config values can be either:

- a single object, which denotes a single bar for that monitor,
- an array of multiple objects, which denotes multiple for that monitor.

Providing this option overrides the single, global `bar` option.

name.description:
A unique identifier for the bar, used for controlling it over IPC.
If not set, uses a generated integer suffix.

**Default**: `bar-n`

popup_autohide.description:
Whether to enable autohide behaviour on the popup.

When enabled, clicking outside the popup will close it.
On some compositors, this may also aggressively steal mouse/keyboard focus.

**Default**: `false`

popup_gap.description:
The size of the gap in pixels
between the bar and the popup window.

**Default**: `5`

position.description:
The bar's position on screen. **Valid options**: `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`
**Default**: `bottom`

start.description:
An array of modules to append to the start of the bar.
Depending on the orientation, this is either the top of the left edge.

**Default**: `[]`

start_hidden.description:
Whether the bar should be hidden when Ironbar starts.

**Default**: `false`, unless `autohide` is set.

disabled.description:
The value of `{adapter_status}` formatting token when adapter is disabled.

**Default**: `"Bluetooth disabled"`

disabling.description:
The value of `{adapter_status}` formatting token when adapter is disabling.

**Default**: `"Disabling Bluetooth..."`

enabled.description:
The value of `{adapter_status}` formatting token when adapter is enabled.

**Default**: `"Bluetooth enabled"`

enabling.description:
The value of `{adapter_status}` formatting token when adapter is enabling.

**Default**: `"Enabling Bluetooth..."`

not_found.description:
The value of `{adapter_status}` formatting token when adapter not found.

**Default**: `"No Bluetooth adapters found"`

BatteryModule.description:
A set of named value threshold-based profiles,
including a default fallback.

Modules using this struct should apply `#[serde(flatten)]`
to the field to avoid `profiles.profiles` syntax.

`S` = State, `T` = Configuration data.

class.description:
Sets one or more CSS classes,
allowing you to target it in CSS using `.class`.

Unlike [name](#name), the `class` property is not expected to be unique.

**Default**: `null`

disable_popup.description: Prevents the popup from opening on-click for this widget.

format.description:
The format string to use for the widget button label.
For available tokens, see [below](#formatting-tokens).

**Default**: `{percentage}%`

icon_size.description:
The size to render the icon at, in pixels.

**Default**: `24`

justify.description:
The justification (alignment) of the widget text shown on the bar. **Valid options**: `left`, `right`, `center`, `fill`
**Default**: `left`

name.description:
Sets the unique widget name,
allowing you to target it in CSS using `#name`.

It is best practise (although not required) to ensure that the value is
globally unique throughout the Ironbar instance
to avoid clashes.

**Default**: `null`

on_click_left.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is left-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_left = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_left_double.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is double-left-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_left_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_middle.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is middle-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_middle = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_middle_double.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is double-middle-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_middle_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_right.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is right-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` /// # Example ```corn { on_click_right = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_right_double.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is double-right-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_right_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

on_mouse_enter.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the cursor begins hovering over the module. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_mouse_enter = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_mouse_exit.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the cursor stops hovering over the module. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_mouse_exit = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_scroll_down.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is scrolled down on. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_scroll_down = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_scroll_up.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is scrolled up on. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_scroll_up = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

orientation.description:
The orientation to display the widget contents. Setting to vertical will rotate text 90 degrees. **Valid options**: `horizontal`, `vertical`
**Default**: `horizontal`

profiles.description:
A map of named profiles against profile entries.
Entries can be provided in two formats.

The profile entry object differs per-module-
check module property documentation to see if they are supported.

See [profiles](/guides/profiles) for more information.

show_if.description:
Shows the module only if the dynamic boolean evaluates to true.

This allows for modules to be dynamically shown or hidden
based on custom events.

**Default**: `null`

smooth_scroll_speed.description:
A multiplier from `0.0` - `10.0` to control the speed
of smooth scrolling on trackpad.

**Default**: `1.0`

tooltip.description:
Shows this text on hover.
Supports embedding scripts between `{{double braces}}`.

Note that full dynamic string support is not currently supported.

**Default**: `null`

transition_duration.description:
The length in milliseconds
of the transition animation to use when showing/hiding the widget.

Note this has no effect if `show_if` is not configured.

**Default**: `250`

transition_type.description:
The transition animation to use when showing/hiding the widget. Note this has no effect if `show_if` is not configured. **Valid options**: `slide_start`, `slide_end`, `crossfade`, `none`
**Default**: `slide_start`

use_default_profiles.description: Whether the default profile map should be merged in.

**Default**: `backlight`

MarqueeMode.description:
Some modules provide options for scrolling text (marquee effect).
This is controlled using a common `MarqueeMode` type,
which is defined below.

enable.description:
Whether to enable scrolling on long lines of text.
This may not be supported by all modules.

**Default**: `false`

max_length.description:
The maximum length of text (roughly, in characters) before it gets truncated and starts scrolling.

**Default**: `null`

on_hover.description:
Controls marquee behavior on hover.

**Options**:
- `"none"`: Always scroll (default)
- `"pause"`: Pause scrolling on hover
- `"play"`: Only scroll on hover

**Default**: `"none"`

pause_duration.description:
Duration in milliseconds to pause at each loop point.

**Default**: `5000` (5 seconds)

scroll_speed.description:
Scroll speed in pixels per frame.
Higher values scroll faster.

**Default**: `0.5`

separator.description:
String displayed between the end and beginning of text as it loops.

**Default**: `"    "` (4 spaces)

MarqueeOnHover.description: Defines the behavior of marquee scrolling on hover.

oneOf.description: Scrolling is always active, hover has no effect.
Scrolling pauses when the widget is hovered.
Scrolling only occurs when the widget is hovered.

You can find the type of the devices on your system by running `nmcli device status`.

ProfileEntry.description:
Represents a single entry in the `profiles` map.

NOTE: Enum variant order matters here.
Serde will attempt to match in order,
which will incorrectly resolve all objects as `Simple`
due to `Default` constraint.

anyOf.description:
A full profile object with configuration attached.

# Example

```corn
{ profiles.example.format = "hello" }
```

A state-matcher only to add styling classes
without any configuration attached.

# Example

```corn
{ profiles.example = 42 }
```

TruncateMode.description:
Some modules provide options for truncating text.
This is controlled using a common `TruncateMode` type,
which is defined below.

The option can be configured in one of two modes.

**Default**: `Auto (end)`

anyOf.description:
Do not truncate content.

Setting this option may cause excessively long content to overflow other widgets,
shifting them off-screen.

# Example

```corn
{ truncate = "off" }

Auto mode lets GTK decide when to ellipsize. To use this mode, set the truncate option to a string declaring the location to truncate text from and place the ellipsis. # Example ```corn { truncate = "start" } ``` **Valid options**: `start`, `middle`, `end`
**Default**: `end`

Length mode defines a fixed point at which to ellipsize.

Generally you will want to set only one of `length` or `max_length`,
but you can set both if required.

# Example

```corn
{
    truncate.mode = "start"
    truncate.length = 50
    truncate.max_length = 70
}
```

length.description:
The fixed width (in characters) of the widget.

The widget will be expanded to this width
if it would have otherwise been smaller.

Leave unset to let GTK automatically handle.

**Default**: `null`

max_length.description:
The maximum number of characters to show
before truncating.

Leave unset to let GTK automatically handle.

**Default**: `null`

mode.description:
The location to truncate text from and place the ellipsis. **Valid options**: `start`, `middle`, `end`
**Default**: `null`

it will fall back to using its actual name.

A [script](scripts) to run when the module is left-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_left = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_left_double.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is double-left-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_left_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_middle.description:

**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_middle = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_middle_double.description:

**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_middle_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_right.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is right-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` /// # Example ```corn { on_click_right = "echo 'event' >> log.txt" } ```

on_click_right_double.description:
A [script](scripts) to run when the module is double-right-clicked. **Supported script types**: `oneshot`.
**Default**: `null` # Example ```corn { on_click_right_double = "echo 'double click' >> log.txt" } ```

orientation.description:
The orientation to display the widget contents. Setting to vertical will rotate text 90 degrees. **Valid options**: `horizontal`, `vertical`
**Default**: `horizontal`

show_if.description:
Shows the module only if the dynamic boolean evaluates to true.

This allows for modules to be dynamically shown or hidden
based on custom events.

**Default**: `null`

smooth_scroll_speed.description:
A multiplier from `0.0` - `10.0` to control the speed
of smooth scrolling on trackpad.

**Default**: `1.0`

sort.description:
The method used for sorting workspaces. - `added` always appends to the end. - `label` sorts by displayed value. - `name` sorts by workspace name. **Valid options**: `added`, `label`, `name`.
**Default**: `label`

tooltip.description:
Shows this text on hover.
Supports embedding scripts between `{{double braces}}`.

Note that full dynamic string support is not currently supported.

**Default**: `null`

transition_duration.description:
The length in milliseconds
of the transition animation to use when showing/hiding the widget.

Note this has no effect if `show_if` is not configured.

**Default**: `250`

transition_type.description:
The transition animation to use when showing/hiding the widget. Note this has no effect if `show_if` is not configured. **Valid options**: `slide_start`, `slide_end`, `crossfade`, `none`
**Default**: `slide_start`

categories.description: XDG categories the associated submenu should contain.

icon.description: Name of the image icon to show next to the label.

label.description: Text to display on the button.
